title: Aspirin cardiovascular prevention
text: Low-dose aspirin reduces the risk of myocardial infarction by approximately 23% in high-risk patients. Daily aspirin use is associated with a 1.6-fold increased risk of major gastrointestinal bleeding. The absolute benefit of aspirin depends on the baseline cardiovascular risk of the patient.

title: COVID-19 mRNA vaccine efficacy
text: The BNT162b2 mRNA vaccine demonstrated 95% efficacy against COVID-19 in the pivotal Phase 3 trial with 43,548 participants. Vaccine efficacy was consistent across age groups, sexes, and diverse ethnic groups. Two doses separated by 21 days are required to achieve maximum protection.

title: Type 2 diabetes metformin first-line
text: Metformin is recommended as first-line pharmacotherapy for type 2 diabetes by major guidelines worldwide. The UKPDS demonstrated that intensive blood glucose control with metformin reduced diabetes-related endpoints by 32% compared to conventional treatment. Metformin reduces HbA1c by approximately 1-2% when used as monotherapy.

title: Statin LDL lowering efficacy
text: High-intensity statin therapy lowers LDL cholesterol by 50% or more. Rosuvastatin 20-40mg reduces LDL by approximately 55% on average. Meta-analysis shows each 1 mmol/L reduction in LDL reduces major cardiovascular events by 22%.

title: Hypertension treatment targets
text: Treating hypertension to a systolic blood pressure target below 130 mmHg reduces stroke risk by 27% compared to less intensive targets. The SPRINT trial found that intensive blood pressure lowering reduced cardiovascular events by 25% and all-cause mortality by 27%. Approximately 30% of adults worldwide have hypertension.

title: Smoking cessation health benefits
text: Smokers who quit before age 40 reduce their excess risk of death associated with continued smoking by approximately 90%. Varenicline increases smoking cessation rates by approximately 2.3-fold compared with placebo at 12 months. Within 1 year of quitting, cardiovascular risk falls to half that of a continuing smoker.

title: Physical activity guidelines
text: Adults should perform at least 150-300 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week according to WHO guidelines. Regular physical activity reduces the risk of all-cause mortality by 35% compared to inactivity. Walking 8,000 steps per day is associated with a 51% lower risk of all-cause mortality.

title: Colorectal cancer screening
text: Colonoscopy reduces colorectal cancer mortality by approximately 68%. Annual fecal immunochemical testing (FIT) has a sensitivity of 79% for colorectal cancer. Population-based colorectal cancer screening programs can reduce incidence by 33-40%.

title: Antibiotic resistance global burden
text: Antimicrobial resistance caused 1.27 million deaths directly attributable worldwide in 2019. MRSA alone was responsible for 100,000 deaths annually. By 2050, antibiotic resistance could kill 10 million people per year if no action is taken.

title: Depression treatment response rates
text: Approximately 50-60% of patients respond to initial antidepressant therapy. The STAR*D trial found that only 33% of patients achieved remission with the first antidepressant. Cognitive behavioral therapy combined with medication achieves response rates of approximately 60-80%.

title: Childhood vaccination impact
text: Measles vaccination has reduced global measles deaths by 99% since the pre-vaccine era. The pneumococcal conjugate vaccine reduces invasive pneumococcal disease by approximately 70% in children under 5. Universal childhood vaccination programs are estimated to save 3-4 million lives per year globally.

title: Obesity prevalence and metabolic risk
text: The global prevalence of obesity has tripled since 1975, affecting 13% of the world's adult population. Each 5 kg/m2 increase in BMI is associated with a 30% increase in overall mortality. Bariatric surgery achieves sustained weight loss of 25-30% and reduces type 2 diabetes incidence by 80%.
expected_claims: bariatric surgery 25-30% weight loss 80% T2DM reduction

title: Breast cancer screening mammography
text: Mammography screening reduces breast cancer mortality by 20-40% in women aged 40-74. False positive rates for mammography are approximately 10% per screening round. Two-yearly mammography is recommended for average-risk women aged 50-74 by most guidelines.

title: Dementia prevention lifestyle factors
text: Up to 40% of dementia cases worldwide are potentially preventable through modification of 12 risk factors. Physical activity reduces dementia risk by approximately 30%. Hearing loss treatment in midlife could prevent approximately 8% of dementia cases.

title: HIV treatment outcomes
text: Antiretroviral therapy has reduced AIDS-related mortality by 80% since the late 1990s. People living with HIV who achieve viral suppression have life expectancy approaching that of HIV-negative individuals. Pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) reduces HIV acquisition risk by up to 99% when taken consistently.
expected_claims: normal life expectancy with viral suppression

title: Lung cancer and smoking
text: Smoking causes approximately 85% of lung cancer cases. Low-dose CT screening reduces lung cancer mortality by 20% in high-risk current and former smokers aged 50-80. Lung cancer 5-year survival rate is only 19% overall but 60% when detected at localized stage.

title: Mental health global burden
text: Depression affects 280 million people globally, making it the leading cause of disability worldwide. Mental health conditions account for 13% of the global burden of disease. Less than 25% of people with mental health conditions in low-income countries have access to treatment.

title: Hand hygiene infection prevention
text: Handwashing with soap reduces respiratory infections by 21% and gastrointestinal infections by 30%. Healthcare-associated infections affect 7% of patients in high-income countries and 15% in low-income countries. Alcohol-based hand rubs are more effective than soap and water against most healthcare pathogens.

title: Preterm birth global statistics
text: Preterm birth affects 11% of all live births globally, approximately 15 million babies per year. Preterm birth complications are the leading cause of death in children under 5, accounting for approximately 1 million deaths annually. Antenatal corticosteroids for preterm labor reduce neonatal mortality by 30-40%.

title: Oral health disease burden
text: Dental caries affects approximately 2.3 billion people with untreated decay in permanent teeth worldwide. Community water fluoridation reduces dental caries by 25% across all ages. Severe periodontitis affects approximately 11% of the global population and is a risk factor for cardiovascular disease and diabetes.
